records.ety: [G. luppe an iron lump. Cf. Looping.]

defs: A mass of iron in a pasty condition gathered into a ball for the tilt hammer or rolls.

records.ety: [Cf. Ir. & Gael. lub loop, noose, fold, thong, bend, lub to bend, incline.]

defs: A fold or doubling of a thread, cord, rope, etc., through which another thread, cord, etc., can be passed, or which a hook can be hooked into; an eye, as of metal; a staple; a noose; a bight.
quotes: That the probation bear no hinge, nor loop To hang a doubt on.

defs: A small, narrow opening; a loophole.
quotes: And stop all sight-holes, every loop from whence The eye of Reason may pry in upon us.

defs: A curve of any kind in the form of a loop.
A wire forming part of a main circuit and returning to the point from which it starts.
The portion of a vibrating string, air column, etc., between two nodes; -- called also ventral segment.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Looped (lpt); p. pr. & vb. n. Looping.]

defs: To make a loop of or in; to fasten with a loop or loops; -- often with up; as, to loop a string; to loop up a curtain.
